ocr.text: In Table 1, the headers are miRNA input (ng) and Yield (ng), with the corresponding data for each row labeled as follows: for
0.5 ng, the yield is 2664 ng; for 1 ng, the yield is 2791 ng; for 3 ng, the yield is 8165 ng; for 9 ng, the yield is 22736 ng; and for
27 ng, the yield is 40340 ng. Table 2 presents the headers as Detecting Scheme, Linear Range (μg/L), Regression Equation,
Correlation Coefficient (R), and Limit of Detection (μg/L). The data for each detecting scheme is: Method 1 has a linear range
of 0.0001~200 μg/L, a regression equation of Y = 219.42X + 748.61, a correlation coefficient of 0.9903, and a limit of detection
of 0.0001 μg/L; Method 2 has a linear range of 0.0003~200 μg/L, a regression equation of Y = 204.83X + 643.88, a correlation
coefficient of 0.9918, and a limit of detection of 0.0003 μg/L; Method 3 has a linear range of 0.0003~200 μg/L, a regression
equation of Y = 194.17X + 613.61, a correlation coefficient of 0.9919, and a limit of detection of 0.0003 μg/L; Method 4 has a
linear range of 0.002~500 μg/L, a regression equation of Y = 171.42X + 398.51, a correlation coefficient of 0.9905, and a limit
of detection of 0.002 μg/L; Method 5 has a linear range of 0.018~500 μg/L, a regression equation of Y = 158.86X + 236.21, a
correlation coefficient of 0.9913, and a limit of detection of 0.018 μg/L; Method 6 has a linear range of 0.25~250 μg/L, a
regression equation of Y = 0.5415X + 0.6196, a correlation coefficient of 0.9945, and a limit of detection of 0.25 μg/L.
